content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Reviewed all three amendments against the codebase. Amendment fr-4-0dab1450 identifies a genuine observability gap where .ok() swallows all errors indiscriminately, inconsistent with the 40+ locations that distinguish NotFound from other errors. Amendment fr-4-e29e2ea2 proposes helpful documentation for a non-obvious two-layer enforcement strategy. Amendment fr-4-e57cd3d1 misidentifies an intentional, well-documented, and regression-tested design decision as a defect — the code explicitly avoids backfilling legacy rows to prevent mislabeling attempts with the wrong plan version after plan evolution.

## Votes

- `fr-4-0dab1450`: Accept
  Confirmed by code inspection. snapshot_plan_hash() at fs.rs:2568-2573 uses .ok() to collapse all read_snapshot errors into None. The rest of the codebase uses explicit match on e.kind() == ErrorKind::NotFound in 40+ locations (e.g., read_task_runs_from_path at line 2581, read_project_config at line 396). read_snapshot itself can return both std::io::Error (including NotFound, PermissionDenied) and AppError::CorruptRecord for malformed JSON. A corrupt or permission-denied snapshot silently preventing plan_hash auto-population is a real operational hazard with zero diagnostic signal. Logging a warning for non-NotFound errors is low-risk, consistent with established codebase patterns, and directly improves observability.
- `fr-4-e29e2ea2`: Accept
  The two-layer enforcement strategy (deny_unknown_fields provides runtime parse failure triggering journal repair fallback; round-trip tests catch field mismatches at test time) is functional but non-obvious. The existing doc-comment on the start test explains 'the round-trip will lose it' but does not explain why deny_unknown_fields is present or how the two layers interact. The completion test has no such doc-comment at all. A brief doc-comment on the structs explaining that deny_unknown_fields is load-bearing (not incidental) and that the round-trip tests are the companion compile-time enforcement would reduce the risk of a future developer removing the annotation as unnecessary boilerplate. This is a low-cost documentation improvement that directly supports maintainability.
- `fr-4-e57cd3d1`: Reject
  Verified by code inspection: this is an intentional, well-documented design decision, not a bug. Explicit comments at lines 2916-2920 and 3246-3250 explain the invariant: 'persist_plan() can advance snapshot.plan_hash after the original attempt started, so stamping the current snapshot hash would mislabel a v1 attempt as v2. Only new entry creation auto-populates.' Three dedicated regression tests (legacy_open_row_without_plan_hash_not_mislabeled_after_plan_evolution, terminal_replay_legacy_row_without_plan_hash_stays_unset, start_replay_after_plan_evolution_does_not_conflict) verify this behavior at lines 5122-5308. Backfilling legacy null rows with the current snapshot hash would create false lineage — claiming a pre-upgrade attempt ran under a plan version that may not have existed when it started. Null correctly means 'unknown provenance,' which is strictly better than an incorrect hash.
